primary_field: Provisional Application 14 — accumulating innovations since Prov 13 filed 2026-04-12

adjacents.field: patent strategy + Cooperative Defensive Patent Pledge (#2260)

filing_target: TBD — Harrity #1 or Mousilli #2, before Nov 26 2026 conversion deadline for Prov 1

observation: Scribe Prov14 opened. Primary field covers all innovations surfaced since Prov 13 filed 2026-04-12 (App 64/036,646, #2225-#2262, 37 innovations, 27 Crown Jewels). Witness at Level 2 for patent strategy and Pledge structure; Level 3 for Crown Jewel criteria; Level 4 for IP allocation.

Prov 14 has 5 innovations pre-inventoried at B110 K422: #2263 Triple-Redundant Verification Architecture (CJ, pre-tagged), #2264 Commons Licensing Dividend (CJ, pre-tagged), #2265 Commons Admission Protocol (CJ, pre-tagged), #2266 Opt-In Member Documentation with Benefits (CJ, recommend), #2267 Member-Generated Guide Corpus (CJ, recommend). Sub-totals: Chessboard 1 / Open Water 4.

B111-B116 A&A candidates not yet inventoried for Prov 14: (a) SP-21 Tidbit Scribe — verification-discipline ledger with auto-append schema. (b) Three Fates Router (Clotho-Lachesis-Atropos) — pipelined AI session-listener that categorizes traffic and invokes sleeping domain-Scribes. (c) Scribes Cathedral — domain-indexed working memory with triply-redundant witness, 6 levels expandable to 12. (d) Member-Owned Scribes Cathedral — consent-gated shareable Scribe tablets as membership product built on (c), feeding R9 personal preload. (e) Cost-Slasher Claim Ladder — empirically-defensible marketing framework (50%+ conservative / up to 95% aggressive) tied to cost-per-correct-answer metric. (f) Fingerprint-Based Incremental Index Reconciliation (K429) — mtime-diff staleness detection for multi-source indexes with async auto-reconcile in session-start hook. (g) Parallel Preload Multilingual Retrieval (Chapter 2 Mellon / K435) — lang-parameterized knowledge-base where parallel translations are sibling preloads, not replacements. Each needs A&A extraction before filing.

Founder insight (B116): Scribes Cathedral is ALSO a member-facing product — members get their own domain-indexed working memory across their workflows. Huge membership incentive especially for R9 users (Cathedrals amplify R9 accuracy on their personal preload). Sets up a new innovation candidate: Member-Owned Scribes Cathedral. Ecosystem extension: optional consent-gated Scribe-sharing within Guild/Tribe per #2260 Pledge.

K432 shipped by Knight — Pedestal Stake Phase 2 complete. 18/18 tests green. Two new patent-candidate patterns surfaced: (a) Pluggable Multi-Provider Adapter with Env-Var Switch (KYC stub/Middesk/Alloy + funding-portal stub/Republic/Wefunder, factory-in-index pattern, same interface contract); (b) Two-Track Context Badge UX (visual separation between Liana Banyan Member context and Upekrithen Stakeholder context in same app, preserving two-track separation at presentation layer). Both should be A&A-evaluated for Prov 14. Also: client-side PDF certificate generation via jsPDF — fast and serverless, but audit-fragile for legal records; flag for server-side-signed variant before real issuance.

K432 report names specific provider choices: HelloSign over DocuSign for e-signature (rationale: lower startup cost, simpler API, free sandbox no credit card); Middesk + Alloy both implemented as adapter impls awaiting real-API sandbox; funding portal still Founder-pick (StartEngine / Republic / Wefunder). Production blockers before live investors: (1) upekrithen schema must be added to Supabase Exposed Schemas in Settings → API, (2) K431 + K432 migrations must be applied, (3) HelloSign sandbox credentials provisioned to enable real e-sig (currently placeholder checkbox), (4) KYC provider sandbox credentials provisioned, (5) pedestal_issuance_log immutability RLS verified against live DB.

K433 shipped by Knight (commit 84053aa) — K427 Workstream 1 COMPLETE. 22/22 two-track-separation tests passing (K431 7 + K432 7 + K433 8). Six admin panels live at /admin/pedestal-stake: issuance queue, cap-table (anonymized by default with click-reveal), annual-raise rolling 12-mo window, bad-actor results with override/reject dispositions logged to immutable log, compliance CSV export for Form C-U, two-track separation live-audit button. Knight ALSO caught a K432 bug as side-effect: PedestalStakeAdmin was querying public.pedestal_holders instead of upekrithen() schema client; fixed in this session. Serial-dispatch-plus-BRIDLE-Rule-2 pattern validated: each subsequent Knight reads prior Knight's work and verifies.

Two new patent-candidate patterns surfaced in K433 for Prov 14 threshing: (a) Two-Track Separation Live-Audit Admin Button — runs 6 live schema-level checks on demand (upekrithen access, FK rejection on members JOIN, issuance log UPDATE/DELETE rejection, UI entity labeling, voting isolation), converting compliance from test-time to runtime-verifiable-by-operator — candidate #2276 pending Founder ratification. (b) Privacy-First Admin View: Anonymized-by-default Holder IDs with Eye-Icon Click-to-Reveal for regulated-data staff workflows, where the reveal action itself is auditable — candidate #2277 pending Founder ratification. Both fit the Cooperative Defensive Patent Pledge #2260 framework.
canonical_ref: K433 deliverables: admin dashboard + two-track audit

observation: K427 WS1 production-launch blockers (Founder/counsel-action queue, carried forward from K432 list + K433 additions): apply K433 migration (K431+K432 already applied), expose upekrithen schema in Supabase API Settings, select funding portal (StartEngine/Republic/Wefunder), select KYC provider (Middesk/Alloy) production, counsel delivers Form C + OM + subscription agreement + risk factors, provision FINRA-registered intermediary, Mercury banking for Upekrithen LLC live. Adapters pluggable via env-var switch so provider picks change one config line, not code.

K435 completion solidifies candidate #2274 (Parallel Preload Multilingual Retrieval Architecture) for Prov 14. Implementation-ready evidence: librarian_context(lang='es') with sibling-file resolution + explicit lang_fallback reporting shipped in v0.3.0, 4 new tests pass, production Cloud Run endpoint serves the feature. Empirical validation via Phase C multilingual probe: ES-HOT 96.7%, EN-HOT 94.5%, +2.2pp gap, 85% threshold cleared. A&A formal drafting for #2274 should cite v0.3.0 (commits/tag) + probe results file + r9v2_base_es.md translator-note provenance as evidence of reduction-to-practice.
